response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals with 15+ years of experience, there is a critical mismatch with the role's requirements. The job explicitly requires native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is not mentioned on the resume. More significantly, the role requires deep embedded firmware and network security expertise (C programming, Linux kernel modification, MACsec/802.1X/encryption implementation, Layer 2/3 networking), while the candidate's recent career has focused on modern web/cloud development (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS) with minimal C experience since 2015 and no demonstrated security or networking domain knowledge.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience demonstrating adaptability across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven capability in high-performance systems and low-latency architecture (Dunnhumby experience with async I/O and real-time systems)
Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, and mentoring evidenced through workshops and open-source contributions
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency mentioned; job lists this as a required qualification making this a potential disqualifier
Career trajectory shows transition away from C/embedded systems (last C++ work in 2015) toward modern web/cloud stack (TypeScript, Node.js), with no recent low-level embedded firmware experience
Zero demonstrated experience in security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), networking stack (Layer 2/3, SNMP, NETCONF, switches), or embedded networking domain required for this role
